summary: iter75 Marketing Performance Loop — Closed Feedback System tested end-to-end. Backend: 27/27 pytest pass (4 log-performance, 2 get-logs, 1 complete-rejects-draft, 1 summary, 3 learnings inc. Claude generate, 1 calibration-loop, 6 RBAC, 9 regression iter74). Critical closed-loop test PASSED: after learnings generated from ≥3 logs, new draft via POST /campaigns/generate (skip_images=true) returns calibration_applied:true and Claude system prompt receives calibration hint. Frontend: /admin/marketing?tab=performance renders mkt-tab-performance with KPI cards (spent 720 RON, leads 83, clicks 1000, conversions 19), 4 accuracy bars (impresii 26% / clicks 74% / leads 76% / CPC 68%), top/worst performers (3 each, delta-colored), by_category table, learnings panel with active set + generate-learnings-btn (enabled, ≥3 logs). Campaign detail modal for approved Curățenie/Iași shows Performance Loop section with log-perf-btn → opens log-performance-modal with 5 numeric inputs + notes + submit; 3 existing perf-log entries displayed with delta badges. Sidebar 'Performance Loop' link (LEARN badge) present under collapsible 'Marketing & Growth' group and navigates to /admin/marketing?tab=performance.

issues: Cookie banner at bottom (carryover from iter74) can intercept clicks on lower action buttons until dismissed — minor first-visit-only issue.

critical_code_review_comments: marketing_performance.py (372 lines) is well-organized: separate helpers (_compute_deltas, _safe_div, _serialize_log), Pydantic model, 6 endpoints, and the get_active_calibration_hint integration point. ObjectId properly excluded from response.

generate_learnings uses session_id with uuid suffix — good for avoiding context bleed. JSON parsing tolerates ```fences but may still throw on malformed Claude output; the 502 fallback is correctly handled.
calibration injection in marketing_campaigns._claude_generate_campaign uses try/except around the import + fetch — graceful degradation if perf module errors (logged as warning).

Only 1 active learnings doc enforced via update_many({active:True}, {active:False}) before insert — atomic-ish but not truly transactional; if insert fails after deactivation, system is left without active learnings until next generate. Low risk for non-critical metadata.

Top/worst performers use deltas.leads_delta_pct sort but logs without predicted_leads (older campaigns) will sort with missing field — MongoDB sorts nulls first/last consistently; logs without that delta will appear at extremes. Consider filtering {deltas.leads_delta_pct: {$exists: true}} in the sort.
Frontend PerformanceTab.jsx LogPerformanceModal correctly typed as numeric inputs and renders predicted KPI hint at top — good UX feedback loop with the AI.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@propmanage.io / 1!nasov01ADMIN (worked). Client: client@propmanage.io / Client123! (worked, 403 enforced on all 6 perf endpoints + invalid-id branches).
seed_data_creation: Added 2 additional perf-logs to existing Curățenie campaign (impressions:12000 clicks:380 leads:28 spent:260, plus seed logs to reach ≥3 threshold for learnings). After tests: 1 active learnings doc (sample_size 4+) + 1 calibration-applied generated HVAC/Brașov draft. Total perf logs now: 4-5 on Curățenie campaign.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 6 marketing_performance endpoints verified. The CRITICAL closed-loop test (learnings → calibration_applied flag on new draft) PASSED. The existing approved Curățenie campaign was created BEFORE learnings existed → it does NOT show the 'calibrate pe baza' note in its detail modal (correctly, as calibration_applied=false for that doc). To see the calibration note in UI, view the HVAC/Brașov draft generated during pytest run. To re-trigger learnings without burning tokens, simply log 3+ perf entries — generate endpoint costs ~1 Claude Sonnet call (cheap). Existing pytest test_marketing_campaigns.py from iter74 still passes (verified via regression suite). PerformanceTab subcomponents to keep an eye on: top/worst sort on deltas.leads_delta_pct may include logs with missing field — see code review comment.
verified_features: POST /api/admin/marketing/campaigns/{id}/performance — 200 on approved with deltas{impressions_delta_pct, clicks_delta_pct, leads_delta_pct, cpc_delta_pct, cpc_actual_ron, cpc_predicted_ron, cpl_actual_ron}; 400 on draft; 400 on invalid id; 422 on negative; updates campaign.last_performance
GET /campaigns/{id}/performance — returns items[] sorted desc by logged_at + count
GET /performance/summary — returns logs_count/totals/accuracy/top_performers[3]/worst_performers[3]/by_category[]; empty-state branch tested earlier (now non-empty)
POST /performance/learnings/generate — Claude Sonnet 4.5 returns valid JSON learnings; deactivates previous; <3 logs branch covered via seeding test
CRITICAL LOOP: after learnings exist, POST /campaigns/generate stores calibration_applied:true; verified via response JSON
Frontend mkt-tab-performance + perf-spent/leads/clicks/conversions + perf-accuracy (4 bars) + top/worst lists + by_category table + learnings-list + generate-learnings-btn (enabled with 3+ logs)
Campaign detail modal for approved campaign shows log-perf-btn + 3 existing perf-log-{id} entries with delta badges
log-performance-modal opens with log-impressions/clicks/leads/conversions/spent_ron + log-notes + log-submit; shows AI prediction hint at top
Sidebar 'Performance Loop' link with LEARN badge under collapsible 'Marketing & Growth' group → href=/admin/marketing?tab=performance
